title: Routing: one prompt can't serve all input types

body.problem: You have a single LLM prompt handling diverse inputs (e.g., customer service queries). Optimizing the prompt for one type (refund requests) hurts performance on others (technical support). A monolithic prompt becomes a compromise that excels at nothing.
body.solution: Add a classification step that routes each input to a specialized handler. The classifier (LLM or traditional model) categorizes the input, then a dedicated prompt/tool chain handles each category. Example: route easy questions to Haiku (cheap, fast) and hard questions to Sonnet (capable).
body.keyTakeaway: Routing lets you optimize each path independently without cross-type interference. Use it when you have distinct input categories that benefit from different handling.
